untranslatable_reason: Signal requires tracking 'consecutive limit-move days' (a multi-bar state counter), which cannot be expressed as a single-bar condition using only the allowed indicators. Limit-move detection (close at exchange limit) requires domain-specific price limits not available in the indicator set. A precise implementation requires: (1) consecutive bar counter logic, (2) limit-up/limit-down definitions per instrument, and (3) position sizing state (50% reduction on day 3, full exit on day 4). These require stateful backtest engine features beyond indicator-based boolean conditions.
translation_notes: Attempted translation uses high volatility (hvol_20) and strong trend confirmation (adx_14) as proxies for the trending+volatile entry regime. Exit attempts to detect exhaustion via excessive range expansion. However, this does NOT capture the core logic: counting consecutive extreme-move days and executing staged exits (50%/50%) at specific day thresholds. A proper implementation requires a custom backtest function or state machine in the engine.
